functions.name: addBunApp
functions.description: Adds a Bun application to the application model. Bun should be available on the PATH.
functions.remarks:
This method executes the script directly using `bun <script>`. Bun natively runs JavaScript and TypeScript
files so no transpile step is required.
If the application directory contains a `package.json` file, Bun will be added as the default package manager.
When publishing to a container, the default base image is `oven/bun:1` for both the build and runtime stages.
functions.signature: addBunApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, scriptPath: string): BunAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the Bun application.

parameters.name: scriptPath
parameters.description: The path to the script (for example, `server.ts`) relative to `appDirectory` to run.

functions.name: addJavaScriptApp
functions.description: Adds a JavaScript application resource to the distributed application using the specified app directory and run script.
functions.remarks:
If a Dockerfile does not exist in the application's directory, one will be generated
automatically when publishing. The method configures the resource with Node.js defaults and sets up npm
integration.
functions.returns: A resource builder for the newly added JavaScript application resource.
functions.signature: addJavaScriptApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, runScriptName?: string): JavaScriptAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The unique name of the JavaScript application resource. Cannot be null or empty.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the JavaScript application.

parameters.name: runScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the npm script to run when starting the application. Defaults to "dev". Cannot be null or empty.

functions.name: addNextJsApp
functions.description: Adds a Next.js app to the distributed application builder.
functions.remarks:
This method configures the Next.js application for both local development and publishing.
In run mode, it starts the Next.js dev server with the correct port binding.
In publish mode, it generates a multi-stage Dockerfile using Next.js standalone output mode,
which copies `public/`, `.next/standalone/`, and `.next/static/` into a
Node.js runtime container.
The Next.js application must have `output: "standalone"` configured in `next.config.ts`
and a `public/` directory (even if empty) for the published container to build correctly.
The following example creates a Next.js app.
```
var builder = DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.AddNextJsApp("frontend", "./frontend");
builder.Build().Run();
```
functions.signature: addNextJsApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, runScriptName?: string): NextJsAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Next.js app.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the Next.js app.

parameters.name: runScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script that runs the Next.js dev server. Defaults to "dev".

functions.name: addNodeApp
functions.description: Adds a node application to the application model. Node should be available on the PATH.
functions.remarks:
This method executes a Node script directly using `node script.js`. If you want to use a package manager
you can add one and configure the install and run scripts using the provided extension methods.
If the application directory contains a `package.json` file, npm will be added as the default package manager.
functions.signature: addNodeApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, scriptPath: string): NodeAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the node application.

parameters.name: scriptPath
parameters.description: The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.

functions.name: addViteApp
functions.description: Adds a Vite app to the distributed application builder.
functions.signature: addViteApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, runScriptName?: string): ViteAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Vite app.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the Vite app.

parameters.name: runScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script that runs the Vite app. Defaults to "dev".

functions.name: disableBuildValidation
functions.description: Disables deploy-time build validation checks for the Next.js application.
functions.remarks:
By default, `AddNextJsApp` adds publish prerequisite steps that verify
the Next.js configuration (e.g. that `output: "standalone"` is set). Use this method
to suppress those checks when the configuration is set dynamically or via an external
mechanism that cannot be detected by static file inspection.

functions.name: publishAsNodeServer
functions.description: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a standalone Node.js server that runs a built artifact directly.
functions.remarks:
Use this method for frameworks that produce a Node.js server artifact during the build and recommend
running that artifact directly in production rather than invoking a package manager script at runtime.
The application source is still built using the configured package manager and build script; this method
only changes the publish-time runtime container shape.
The container files source path is automatically set to `outputPath` so that only
the built output directory is copied into the runtime container, not the full application source.

parameters.name: entryPoint
parameters.description: The relative path to the Node.js entry point to execute in the published container after the build completes, such as `.output/server/index.mjs` or `build/index.js`.

parameters.name: outputPath
parameters.description: The relative path containing the built runtime files to copy into the published container. Defaults to the application root.

functions.name: publishAsPackageScript
functions.description: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a Node.js server that uses a `package.json` script at runtime.
functions.remarks:
Use this method for frameworks where the production server depends on packages in `node_modules` at runtime.
The resulting container includes the full application with production dependencies installed.
This method is appropriate for frameworks like Nuxt (where `useAsyncData`/`useFetch` requires the
full Nitro environment), Remix (where `react-router-serve` is an npm dependency), and Astro SSR
(where the built entry point imports unbundled `@astrojs/*` packages).
For frameworks that produce a self-contained server artifact that does not require `node_modules`,
use `PublishAsNodeServer``1` instead for a smaller runtime image.

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The name of the `package.json` script to run in the published container. For example, `start` invokes the configured package manager's run command for the `start` script, such as `npm run start`, `pnpm run start`, `yarn run start`, or `bun run start`.

parameters.name: runScriptArguments
parameters.description: Optional arguments appended after the script name at runtime, such as `-- --port "$PORT"`.

functions.name: publishAsStaticWebsite
functions.description: Publishes the JavaScript application as a standalone static website using YARP.
functions.signature: publishAsStaticWebsite(apiPath?: string, apiTarget?: IResourceWithServiceDiscovery, outputPath?: string, stripPrefix?: boolean, targetEndpointName?: string): JavaScriptAppResource

functions.name: withBrowserDebugger
functions.description: Configures a browser debugger for the JavaScript application resource, enabling browser-based debugging through a child resource that launches when the parent application is ready.
functions.remarks:
This method creates a child `BrowserDebuggerResource` that waits for the parent JavaScript
application to start, then launches a browser debug session targeting the parent's HTTP or HTTPS endpoint.
The parent resource must have at least one HTTP or HTTPS endpoint configured.

parameters.name: browser
parameters.description: The browser to use for debugging. Defaults to `"msedge"`. Supported values include `"msedge"` and `"chrome"`.

functions.name: withBuildScript
functions.description: Adds a build script annotation to the resource builder using the specified command-line arguments.
functions.remarks:
Use this method to specify custom build scripts for JavaScript application resources during
deployment.
functions.returns: The same resource builder instance with the build script annotation applied.

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to be executed when the resource is built.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: An array of command-line arguments to use for the build script.

functions.name: withBun
functions.description: Configures the JavaScript resource to use Bun as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: installArgs
parameters.description: Additional command-line arguments passed to "bun install". When null, defaults are applied based on publish mode and lockfile presence.

functions.name: withNpm
functions.description: Configures the Node.js resource to use npm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
functions.signature: withNpm(install?: boolean, installCommand?: string, installArgs?: string[]): JavaScriptAppResource

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: installCommand
parameters.description: The install command itself passed to npm to install dependencies.

parameters.name: installArgs
parameters.description: The command-line arguments passed to npm to install dependencies.

functions.name: withPnpm
functions.description: Configures the Node.js resource to use pnpm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: installArgs
parameters.description: The command-line arguments passed to "pnpm install".

functions.name: withRunScript
functions.description: Adds a run script annotation to the specified JavaScript application resource builder, specifying the script to execute and its arguments during run mode.
functions.remarks:
Use this method to specify a custom script and its arguments that should be executed when the resource is executed
in RunMode.
functions.returns: The same resource builder instance with the run script annotation applied, enabling further configuration.

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to be executed when the resource is run.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: An array of arguments to pass to the script.

functions.name: withViteConfig
functions.description: Configures the Vite app to use the specified Vite configuration file instead of the default resolution behavior.
functions.remarks: Use this method to specify a specific Vite configuration file if you need to override the default Vite configuration resolution behavior.

parameters.name: configPath
parameters.description: The path to the Vite configuration file. Relative to the Vite service project root.

functions.name: withYarn
functions.description: Configures the Node.js resource to use yarn as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: installArgs
parameters.description: The command-line arguments passed to "yarn install".

handleTypes.name: BunAppResource
handleTypes.description: A resource that represents a Bun application.

handleTypes.name: JavaScriptAppResource
handleTypes.description: A resource that represents a JavaScript application.

capabilities.name: publishAsNodeServer
capabilities.description: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a standalone Node.js server that runs a built artifact directly.
capabilities.remarks:
Use this method for frameworks that produce a Node.js server artifact during the build and recommend
running that artifact directly in production rather than invoking a package manager script at runtime.
The application source is still built using the configured package manager and build script; this method
only changes the publish-time runtime container shape.
The container files source path is automatically set to `outputPath` so that only
the built output directory is copied into the runtime container, not the full application source.

parameters.name: entryPoint
parameters.description: The relative path to the Node.js entry point to execute in the published container after the build completes, such as `.output/server/index.mjs` or `build/index.js`.

parameters.name: outputPath
parameters.description: The relative path containing the built runtime files to copy into the published container. Defaults to the application root.

capabilities.name: publishAsPackageScript
capabilities.description: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a Node.js server that uses a `package.json` script at runtime.
capabilities.remarks:
Use this method for frameworks where the production server depends on packages in `node_modules` at runtime.
The resulting container includes the full application with production dependencies installed.
This method is appropriate for frameworks like Nuxt (where `useAsyncData`/`useFetch` requires the
full Nitro environment), Remix (where `react-router-serve` is an npm dependency), and Astro SSR
(where the built entry point imports unbundled `@astrojs/*` packages).
For frameworks that produce a self-contained server artifact that does not require `node_modules`,
use `PublishAsNodeServer``1` instead for a smaller runtime image.

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The name of the `package.json` script to run in the published container. For example, `start` invokes the configured package manager's run command for the `start` script, such as `npm run start`, `pnpm run start`, `yarn run start`, or `bun run start`.

parameters.name: runScriptArguments
parameters.description: Optional arguments appended after the script name at runtime, such as `-- --port "$PORT"`.

capabilities.remarks:
This method creates a child `BrowserDebuggerResource` that waits for the parent JavaScript
application to start, then launches a browser debug session targeting the parent's HTTP or HTTPS endpoint.
The parent resource must have at least one HTTP or HTTPS endpoint configured.

parameters.name: browser
parameters.description: The browser to use for debugging. Defaults to `"msedge"`. Supported values include `"msedge"` and `"chrome"`.
